title: Carrington Event
subtitle: Northern lights visible from Cuba
fact: In 1859, during the Carrington Event, the Northern Lights were so bright that people in Cuba could read newspapers at midnight.

title: Which Planet Has the Most Moons?
subtitle: Saturn — 146 confirmed moons
fact: Saturn has 146 confirmed moons — more than any other planet in the Solar System. Jupiter is second with 95.

title: A Day on Venus
subtitle: Longer than a year
fact: A day on Venus (243 Earth days) is longer than its year (225 Earth days). It also rotates backwards.

title: Footprints on the Moon
subtitle: They'll last millions of years
fact: The footprints left by Apollo astronauts on the Moon will remain for at least 10 million years — there's no wind or water to erase them.

title: The Sun is Huge
subtitle: 1.3 million Earths fit inside
fact: About 1.3 million Earths could fit inside the Sun. Yet it's just an average-sized star.

title: Sunsets on Mars
subtitle: They're blue, not red
fact: On Mars, sunsets appear blue. The planet's thin, dusty atmosphere scatters light differently than on Earth.

title: No One Can Hear You
subtitle: Space has no sound
fact: In space, no one can hear you scream. Sound waves need a medium to travel through — and space is a vacuum.

title: Jupiter's Great Red Spot
subtitle: A storm bigger than Earth
fact: Jupiter's Great Red Spot is a storm larger than Earth that has been raging for at least 350 years.

title: Neutron Stars
subtitle: A sugar cube weighs billions of tons
fact: A single teaspoon of neutron star material would weigh about 10 million tons — as much as a mountain.

title: Gold Comes From Space
subtitle: Forged in neutron star collisions
fact: The gold on Earth was formed in neutron star collisions billions of years ago. We are literally wearing stardust.

title: Sagittarius A*
subtitle: 4 million solar masses
fact: At the center of our Milky Way galaxy lies Sagittarius A*, a supermassive black hole with the mass of 4 million Suns.

title: Pluto Has a Heart
subtitle: A giant glacier in the shape of a heart
fact: Pluto has a heart-shaped glacier called Tombaugh Regio, discovered by New Horizons in 2015. It's made of nitrogen ice.

title: International Space Station
subtitle: Travels at 28,000 km/h
fact: The ISS orbits Earth at 28,000 km/h — fast enough to see a sunrise every 90 minutes. It completes 16 orbits per day.

title: Hubble Deep Field
subtitle: 10,000 galaxies in a tiny speck
fact: The Hubble Deep Field captured 10,000 galaxies in a patch of sky so small it would take 100 grains of sand to cover it.

title: Voyager's Golden Record
subtitle: Earth's message to the cosmos
fact: Voyager 1 and 2 carry Golden Records — phonograph records with sounds and images of Earth, in case aliens find them.

title: The Speed of Light
subtitle: It takes 8 minutes from the Sun
fact: Light from the Sun takes 8 minutes and 20 seconds to reach Earth. The nearest star, Proxima Centauri, is 4.2 light-years away.

title: Recycled Water in Space
subtitle: Astronauts drink recycled urine
fact: The ISS recycles 93% of all water — including urine and sweat. Astronauts drink water that was someone else's yesterday.

title: Coldest Place in the Universe
subtitle: Boomerang Nebula: -272°C
fact: The Boomerang Nebula is the coldest known place in the universe at -272°C — just 1 degree above absolute zero.

title: Where Is Most Water?
subtitle: Not on Earth
fact: Europa, one of Jupiter's moons, is believed to have more liquid water than all of Earth's oceans combined.

title: The Moon Is Drifting Away
subtitle: 3.8 cm per year
fact: The Moon is moving away from Earth at 3.8 centimeters per year. In 600 million years, total solar eclipses will be impossible.

title: Oldest Star Map
subtitle: Nebra Sky Disk
fact: The Nebra Sky Disk, dated to 1600 BC, is the oldest known depiction of the cosmos — a bronze disk with the Sun, Moon, and stars.

title: Echo of the Big Bang
subtitle: Cosmic Microwave Background
fact: The faint glow of the Big Bang, called the Cosmic Microwave Background, was discovered by accident in 1965 and fills all of space.

title: Most Expensive Material
subtitle: Antimatter costs $62.5 trillion per gram
fact: Antimatter is the most expensive substance in the universe — it costs about $62.5 trillion to produce one gram.

title: Loneliest Object in the Universe
subtitle: The Bootes Void
fact: The Boötes Void is a spherical region of space 330 million light-years across that contains almost no galaxies at all.

title: Stars Create Elements
subtitle: We are made of star stuff
fact: Every atom of carbon, oxygen, and iron in your body was formed inside a star and scattered across the universe by a supernova.

title: Astronauts Grow Taller
subtitle: Up to 5 cm taller in space
fact: Without gravity compressing the spine, astronauts can grow up to 5 cm taller during their stay on the ISS.

title: Most of the Solar System is Empty
subtitle: Vast emptiness between planets
fact: If you shrunk the Sun to the size of a grain of salt, the Solar System would be the size of a city — almost completely empty.

title: One Small Step
subtitle: First words on the Moon
fact: Neil Armstrong's famous "one small step" quote was improvised. After landing he said: "That's one small step for man, one giant leap for mankind."

title: Halley's Comet
subtitle: Returns every 76 years
fact: Halley's Comet visits the inner Solar System every 76 years. It will return in 2061 — Mark Twain was born and died during its visits.

title: Most Volcanic Place
subtitle: Io — Jupiter's volcanic moon
fact: Jupiter's moon Io is the most volcanically active body in the Solar System — it has over 400 active volcanoes erupting constantly.

title: The Universe Is 13.8 Billion Years Old
subtitle: Exactly measured by Planck
fact: The universe is 13.8 billion years old. Light from the most distant galaxies has been traveling toward us for almost that long.

title: First Photo From Space
subtitle: A V-2 rocket captured this
fact: The first photograph of Earth from space was taken in 1946 by a camera mounted on a captured German V-2 rocket at 105 km altitude.
